name: La Tierra de Ordás – running circular desde Santa María de Ordás
region_intro: Jogging around Santa María De Ordás offers a variety of routes through the rural landscape of León. The region is characterized by its rolling hills and valleys, with routes often traversing open countryside and forested sections. Elevation gains are common, providing challenging ascents and rewarding views. The terrain primarily consists of natural paths and tracks, suitable for trail running. Best jogging routes around Santa María De Ordás The most popular jogging route is Cruz de Fontañán – Alto de Monterrey loop from Olleros de Alba, a 10.8 miles (17.4 km) trail that takes 2 hours 52 minutes to complete, featuring significant elevation gain through varied terrain. Another top favourite among local runners is The Drovers' Roads Trail, a difficult 9.7 miles (15.7 km) path. This route follows historical paths, offering a mix of open landscapes and rural scenery. Local runners also love the La Tierra de Ordás – running circular desde Santa María de Ordás, a 7.5 miles (12.1 km) trail leading through the local countryside, often completed in about 1 hour 29 minutes. Jogging around Santa María De Ordás is defined by its hilly terrain, rural paths, and occasional forested sections. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate runs to more challenging routes with substantial climbs. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 50 runners have used komoot to explore Santa María De Ordás's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on running routes in Santa María De Ordás?
A: The running routes around Santa María De Ordás primarily feature natural paths and tracks, suitable for trail running. You'll encounter a mix of rolling hills, valleys, open countryside, and occasional forested sections. Elevation gains are common, providing both challenging ascents and rewarding views.

Q: Are there any easy or beginner-friendly running trails in Santa María De Ordás?
A: While many routes in Santa María De Ordás involve significant elevation, there are moderate options available. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1052018123/ruta-de-los-calderones-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ruta de los Calderones Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 6 km (3.7 miles) route that offers a good introduction to the area's terrain without being overly strenuous.

Q: Where can I find longer distance running trails in the region?
A: For longer distances and more challenging runs, Santa María De Ordás offers several options. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31455073" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cruz de Fontañán – Alto de Monterrey loop from Olleros de Alba&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 17.4 km (10.8 miles) trail with substantial elevation gain. Another demanding option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1055989830/the-drovers-roads-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Drovers' Roads Trail&lt;/a&gt;, which spans 15.7 km (9.7 miles) and follows historical paths.

Q: Are there any circular running routes or loops in Santa María De Ordás?
A: Yes, there are several circular running routes. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1055974406/la-tierra-de-ordas-running-circular-desde-santa-maria-de-ordas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Tierra de Ordás – running circular desde Santa María de Ordás&lt;/a&gt;, which is a 12.1 km (7.5 miles) loop through the local countryside. Another challenging circular route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31455045" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cruz de Fontañán loop from Olleros de Alba&lt;/a&gt;, covering 11.8 km (7.3 miles).

Q: What scenic attractions or landmarks can I see while running in Santa María De Ordás?
A: The region offers several natural and historical points of interest. You might encounter the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4073598" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Calderones Gorge&lt;/a&gt; or the beautiful &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5558248" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Waterfall of the Arroyo de los Calderones&lt;/a&gt;. For historical sights, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5558242" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of the Virgin of Manadero in the Cave of the Doves&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7187739" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camposagrado Bell Tower&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other runners say about the trails in Santa María De Ordás?
A: The running trails in Santa María De Ordás are well-regarded by the komoot community, with over 50 runners having explored the varied terrain. Users often praise the challenging nature of the routes, the significant elevation gains, and the rural scenery that defines the region.

Q: Are the running routes in Santa María De Ordás suitable for families?
A: Many routes in Santa María De Ordás feature hilly terrain and natural paths, which might be challenging for very young children or strollers. However, families with older children who are accustomed to trail running or hiking may enjoy the moderate routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1052018123/ruta-de-los-calderones-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ruta de los Calderones Trail&lt;/a&gt;, which is shorter and less strenuous.

Q: Are there any dog-friendly running trails in Santa María De Ordás?
A: Generally, the natural paths and open countryside of Santa María De Ordás are suitable for running with dogs. However, it's always advisable to keep dogs on a leash, especially when passing through agricultural areas or near livestock, and to respect local regulations regarding pets in natural parks or protected areas.

Q: What is the best time of year to go running in Santa María De Ordás?
A: The best time for running in Santa María De Ordás is typically during spring and autumn when temperatures are milder and the natural landscape is at its most vibrant. Summer can be warm, so early morning or late evening runs are recommended. Winter running is possible, but some trails might be muddy or slippery, especially at higher elevations.

Q: How many running routes are available in Santa María De Ordás?
A: There are at least 5 highly-rated running routes available around Santa María De Ordás, offering a mix of moderate and difficult options to suit different fitness levels and preferences.

Q: Are there any challenging running routes for experienced runners?
A: Absolutely. Santa María De Ordás is known for its challenging terrain. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31455073" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cruz de Fontañán – Alto de Monterrey loop from Olleros de Alba&lt;/a&gt;, with over 1000 meters of elevation gain, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1055989830/the-drovers-roads-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Drovers' Roads Trail&lt;/a&gt; are classified as difficult and will test even seasoned runners.
